title: Disconnect leaves hero stuck on remote card — slow feed tick clobbers the local paint
description:
User pressed Disconnect; the hero footer stayed 'remote · online · <PodGPU>' instead of flipping to 'local · offline · <local GPU>'. Settings panel correctly showed 'stopped' + label 'Connect', but the footer was frozen on the Pod card (self-corrected only ~15-70s later, or when the user did something else like Connect).

Hero engine has 4 stages (heroStats.js _renderEngine): local·offline, connecting·offline (live %), remote·online (Pod GPU card), disconnecting·online (blank slot); + a hidden sticky 'disconnected' for involuntary drops. Disconnect cycle is remote → disconnecting → local. This bug = the disconnecting→local hop was clobbered back to remote.

ROOT (race): the app-wide connection feed (js/shell.js tick) samples /remote/comfy/status as connected:true (Pod still up), then AWAITS /remote/pod/specs (~0.5s) before emitting the Pod card. If the user's Disconnect finally-emit (MpiRunpodSettings.js:835, connected:false phase:null → local paint) lands DURING that await, the tick then emits the now-stale connected:true Pod card at shell.js:1135 and clobbers the local paint. Feed-local _last is then true, so the miss-debounce (shell.js:1083, _misses<MISS_THRESHOLD && _last) suppresses recovery for ~3 ticks × 5-30s backoff — hence 'frozen' until something forces it.

Second, latent asymmetry: the feed's !connected ladder had stale-'connecting' recovery (two spots) but NO stale-'disconnecting' recovery, so a lost/raced disconnect finally-emit could also leave the hero stuck on 'disconnecting · online'.

FIX (js/shell.js): (1) after the specs await, re-read the shared _remotePhase — if it became 'disconnecting', the teardown is authoritative → DROP the stale connected:true emit (return). (2) Add stale-'disconnecting' recovery to the !connected ladder (mirror the existing 'connecting' one): backend reports not-connected while phase==='disconnecting' → resolve to clean local·offline.

Distinct from MPI-239 (untracked Pod DEATH self-heal, server-side). This is the user-initiated Disconnect race, renderer-side. NOT live-verified — user to test by pressing Disconnect and watching the footer flip to local immediately.
